meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't collaborate. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve Collaboration Issues
content.seo_description: Overcome collaboration bottlenecks with a professional SaaS starter kit. Learn to use Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your freelance delivery.
content.answer_block: Collaboration failures occur when project architecture lacks standardization. By deploying a pre-configured SaaS starter kit, you unify your environment, enforce version control, and synchronize workflows. Combining Antigravity's rapid scaffolding with Claude Code’s iterative intelligence allows you to ship production-ready, modular SaaS apps in hours, not weeks, ensuring seamless client handoffs.
content.intro:
You’ve been there: the client is waiting, the scope is creeping, and your codebase feels like a sprawling, unnavigable labyrinth. When collaboration stalls, your reputation as a high-velocity developer takes the hit. The bottleneck isn't your talent; it’s the lack of a standardized foundation that prevents others from jumping in and contributing effectively.

Today, we stop building from scratch. We are pivoting to a modular, starter-kit-first architecture. By integrating Antigravity for structural integrity and Claude Code for intelligent implementation, you’ll transform your workflow from a solo struggle into a machine that delivers scalable SaaS products with surgical precision.

steps.title: Initialize SaaS Foundation
steps.description: Establish a robust directory structure that separates concerns, ensuring your UI, API, and database schemas are decoupled for easy team access.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular SaaS starter kit architecture including Next.js, Supabase, and Tailwind CSS, ensuring a clean separation between client and server logic.

steps.title: Enforce Versioning Protocols
steps.description: Standardize branch naming and commit messages to ensure that any developer joining the project can immediately identify the status of features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a git-flow schema and husky pre-commit hooks that enforce conventional commits and linting standards across the repo.

steps.title: Automate Documentation Synthesis
steps.description: Use AI to generate living documentation that updates as you code, preventing the 'collaboration gap' caused by outdated README files.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an automated script that scans the codebase and updates the README.md with current API endpoints and environment variable requirements.

steps.title: Implement Role-Based Access Control
steps.description: Secure the collaboration environment by defining clear permission levels for different stakeholders using your starter kit's auth layer.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a role-based access control (RBAC) middleware for the API that restricts sensitive routes based on user roles defined in the Supabase schema.

steps.title: Deploy Continuous Integration
steps.description: Set up an automated pipeline that validates code quality before any merge, ensuring the codebase remains stable for all collaborators.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Setup a GitHub Actions workflow that triggers unit tests and build checks on every pull request to the main branch.

pro_tips: Always define a 'Global Constants' file early to prevent hardcoded strings from causing integration bugs during handoffs.
Use Claude Code to create a 'Decision Log' file that tracks why specific architectural choices were made, saving hours of explanation for new collaborators.
Implement an .env.example file with dummy values for every single environment variable to ensure zero onboarding friction for team members.
Leverage Antigravity to scaffold unit tests alongside your features; a tested codebase is significantly easier for others to modify without breaking.
Adopt a 'Feature-Flag' approach to development, allowing you to merge incomplete code into the main branch without disrupting the stable production environment.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is live, invite a peer to clone the repository and attempt to deploy a new feature using the documentation you generated in Step 3. If they can complete it without your intervention, your collaboration system is production-ready.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your own code and start scaling your freelance business with a system that works for you. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
